audit: Cross-reference of all reviewer comments vs. fixes applied

items.reviewer: How exactly is h_i computed — does it include predecessor's hash?
items.evidence: models.py _compute_hash() now includes _prev_hash. Definition 1 updated in both MD and LaTeX to h_i = SHA-256(σ(e_i) || h_{i-1}).

items.reviewer: Why is 'most recent validator's confidence' chosen over aggregations?
items.evidence: Added as Limitation #1 in §7.4 and Future Work item. Paper now acknowledges LWW semantics explicitly.

items.reviewer: CRDT claim is underdeveloped; status/confidence not commutative/associative. Need formal proofs or qualification.
items.evidence: Changed 'CRDT-like' → 'CRDT-inspired' throughout both MD and LaTeX. Added explicit LWW acknowledgment. Added 'Formal CRDT proofs' to Future Work.

items.reviewer: How is 0.90 Jaccard threshold selected? Sensitivity analysis needed.
items.evidence: New §6.5 Fork Threshold Sensitivity. Sweep 0.70-0.95, 21 pairs. Zero FP across all thresholds. Added to both MD and LaTeX. Test: test_fork_sensitivity.py

items.reviewer: What is empirical effect of SSA on ambiguity reduction? Benchmark needed.
items.evidence: New benchmark: 29 sentences, P=0.812, R=1.000, F1=0.897. Added to §3.4 in both MD and LaTeX. Test: test_ssa_benchmark.py

items.reviewer: Can you provide concrete RDF/OWL/PROV-O mapping with evaluation?
items.evidence: New §6.6 RDF Mapping Evaluation. Turtle compiler, 5 examples, 100% preservation. Test: test_rdf_mapping.py

items.reviewer: SSA is heuristic with limited coverage; no formal guarantees.
items.evidence: Acknowledged in §3.4 with quantitative benchmark. Three FP categories explicitly listed. Limitations acknowledged.

items.reviewer: Fork resolution via Jaccard seems ad hoc; no sensitivity or FP/FN analysis.
items.evidence: Jaccard formula formally defined in §4.2. Sensitivity sweep in §6.5. Zero FP across all thresholds confirmed.

items.reviewer: AML pipeline underspecified (dataset source, size, ground truth).
items.evidence: Acknowledged as limitation, but actual dataset details (source, license, metrics vs gold schema) remain unspecified. Needs: dataset provenance statement, ground truth annotation, or clarifying that it's a proof-of-concept.

items.reviewer: Missing comparisons: nanopublications, Git-based KG versioning, SHACL, CRDT applications, RDF*/reification.
items.evidence: Nanopublications and CRDT mentioned in §2.3-2.4. Future Work includes SHACL, formal CRDT proofs. BUT: no concrete comparison table or evaluation against these baselines.

items.reviewer: No adversarial or stress tests (AgentPoison-style attacks, malicious forks).
items.evidence: §7.3 Failure Modes discusses adversarial scenarios (tampering, chain poisoning, SSA circumvention). BUT: no actual adversarial experiments run (e.g., simulated malicious agent appending poison events).

items.reviewer: Why event_id is 'UUID4 (12-char hex)' rather than full UUID? Collision risk?
items.evidence: Still uses 12-char hex. A reviewer note says this increases collision probability vs full 36-char UUID. Should either justify (12 chars = 48 bits = 2.8e14 space, negligible for single-concept chains) or change to full UUID.

items.reviewer: Have you considered signing events per-agent (Ed25519) and anchoring checkpoints?
items.evidence: Not mentioned in paper. Could be added as Future Work item alongside 'Formal CRDT proofs' or in the Failure Modes section.

items.reviewer: How does system handle identical timestamps? Vector clocks? Expose non-determinism?
items.evidence: Mentioned in §7.3 Failure Modes (timestamp collisions, secondary sort by hash). BUT: no vector clock or logical clock implementation. No explicit discussion of whether non-determinism is exposed to users.

priority_actions.why: Reviewer explicitly asked. Simple justification: 12 hex chars = 48 bits = ~2.8×10^14 space. For 1M events, birthday paradox collision prob ≈ 1.8×10^-3. Add this math to the paper or switch to full UUID.

priority_actions.action: Add Ed25519 signing to Future Work
priority_actions.why: Reviewer explicitly asked about per-agent signatures and anchoring. Add one line to §7.5 Future Work: 'Event signing (Ed25519 per-agent keys) and periodic Merkle root anchoring to external transparency logs.'

priority_actions.action: Add vector clock discussion to Failure Modes
priority_actions.why: Reviewer asked about identical timestamp handling. Add a sentence to §7.3: 'For production deployments, logical clocks or hybrid logical clocks (HLC) could replace wall-clock timestamps to guarantee causal ordering without relying on synchronized clocks.'

Reviewer asked about provenance, scale, ground truth. Add a sentence to §6.3 E6: 'The AML pipeline uses anonymized transaction data from [source/IBM AML benchmark]; the experiment serves as a proof-of-concept for the import-first methodology rather than a production-scale evaluation.'
